gap_summaries.title: Breaking-hash reuse bypasses factory_version check
gap_summaries.description: The breaking-hash fallback in check_gate_reuse allowed gate reuse when only profile.hash differed but factory_version also changed. A gate from an older factory version could be reused with a newer version that has different adjudication logic.
gap_summaries.fix: Added factory_version equality check alongside breaking_hash comparison. Added test test_factory_version_change_blocks_breaking_hash_reuse.

gap_summaries.title: gate_id not validated in gate_history filename construction
gap_summaries.description: record_gate_result interpolated gate_id directly into a filename without validation, allowing path traversal via crafted gate_ids (e.g., '../escape').
gap_summaries.fix: Added _SAFE_GATE_ID regex validation matching evidence_io._validate_gate_id pattern. Added tests for path traversal and slash rejection.

gap_summaries.title: Bare except Exception swallows all learning-loop errors
gap_summaries.description: The try/except around record_gate_for_learning in route_gate_verdict caught Exception, silently hiding programming bugs (TypeError, AttributeError) alongside expected OS errors.
gap_summaries.fix: Narrowed to except (OSError, ValueError) to still propagate programming errors while not blocking verdict routing on filesystem issues.

gap_summaries.title: Duplicate burn-in proposals inflate calibration counts
gap_summaries.description: propose_burnin_calibrations emitted one CalibrationProposal per flaky category, all targeting the same field (rules.test_quality.burn_in_runs). This inflated applied-count in audit events and retrospective summaries.
gap_summaries.fix: Emit a single proposal with all flaky categories listed in the rationale. Changed category to 'test_quality' since it targets a single field.

gap_summaries.title: _set_nested silently destroys non-dict intermediates
gap_summaries.description: If an intermediate path segment existed but was not a dict (e.g., corrupted profile), _set_nested silently replaced it with an empty dict, destroying the existing value.
gap_summaries.fix: Raise ValueError when an intermediate exists but is not a dict. Added test test_non_dict_intermediate_raises.

gap_summaries.title: compute_category_trends not integrated into compute_gate_metrics
gap_summaries.description: Plan Task 6 specified trends should be integrated via a 'category_trends' key in compute_gate_metrics return dict, but it was omitted.
gap_summaries.fix: Added 'category_trends': compute_category_trends(history) to the return dict.

gap_summaries.title: bump_profile_version does not validate change_type parameter
gap_summaries.description: Any string passed as change_type would silently do a feature bump via the else branch, masking typos.
gap_summaries.fix: Added ValueError guard for invalid change_type values. Added test test_invalid_change_type_rejected.

gap_summaries.title: check_gate_reuse relies on fragile string matching for reason classification
gap_summaries.description: The condition 'profile.hash mismatch' in reason relies on exact wording from can_reuse_gate_file in evidence_io.py. If that message is reformatted, the breaking-hash fallback silently stops working.
gap_summaries.fix_suggestion: Refactor can_reuse_gate_file to return structured reason codes. Deferred: would require modifying evidence_io.py which is an m2 module not owned by this milestone.

gap_summaries.title: load_gate_history has no size limit
gap_summaries.description: No upper bound on records loaded. Mitigated by prune_gate_history capping at 1000 records, but no defensive limit if pruning is skipped.
gap_summaries.rationale: Pruning to max_records=1000 bounds practical size. Adding a limit parameter adds complexity without clear benefit at current scale.

gap_summaries.title: prune_gate_history preserves unparseable files unconditionally
gap_summaries.description: Corrupt JSON files are added to surviving list and never pruned, potentially accumulating over time.
gap_summaries.rationale: Conservative choice — deleting unreadable files could hide data corruption. Unparseable files are unlikely in practice since writes use write_atomic + canonical_json.
